senses.meaning: to move upward / to rise up
senses.specializedMeaning: For the subject or object to <verb> to a higher spatial location, with emphasis on translocation along a vertical or upward-sloping path.

senses.meaning: to be completed / to be finished
senses.specializedMeaning: For the action denoted by <verb> to reach completion, finality, or a fully finished state; the culmination point of a process.

senses.meaning: to swell up / to increase in size or intensity
senses.specializedMeaning: For the subject to <verb> such that something swells, surges, or intensifies in a non-spatial, non-completive sense — excludes upward spatial movement (meaning 1) and excludes processes that simply reach exhaustion or completion (meaning 2); the subject becomes more extreme or pronounced in volume, emotional intensity, or magnitude.

senses.meaning: to come to the surface / to emerge or appear
senses.specializedMeaning: For something previously hidden, internal, contained, or subjacent to <verb> such that it becomes visible, exposed, revealed, or brought to the surface; a change from concealment to manifestation.

senses.meaning: to stick or cling to; to attach to
senses.specializedMeaning: to cause a <verb> action to physically adhere, cling, or affix to a surface or object (resultative: the object of <verb> becomes attached)

senses.meaning: to come to / reach / arrive at
senses.specializedMeaning: to succeed in <verb>-ing through effort or pursuit, reaching or arriving at an achieved goal or a target location

senses.meaning: to settle into a state; to become accustomed to
senses.specializedMeaning: to <verb> into or assume a new state, condition, or habit, with inchoative aspect marking the entry into that state

senses.meaning: to come up with / occur to (someone)
senses.specializedMeaning: to <verb> an idea, solution, or concept spontaneously into consciousness (mental occurrence, not physical action)
